# reality-resi-stack

> Residential-IP-first VLESS + Reality toolkit for sing-box. One-line installer for Ubuntu 22.04+/Debian 12+ that sets up VLESS+Reality+xtls-rprx-vision, a zero-dependency Python subscription server, optional dual-node smart routing (residential for OpenAI/banking/Netflix, data-center fallback for Telegram/Discord), BBR + UFW + fail2ban + systemd-timer daily backups, with idempotent re-runs and a hash-only secret denylist enforced in CI.

Designed for people who *bought* a residential-IP VPS specifically because OpenAI/Anthropic/banks/streaming reward residential egress reputation — but who are then hit by Telegram/Discord soft-throttling that's specific to residential subnets shared with previous bot abusers. Most VLESS installers solve the "cheap VPS bypass" case; this one solves the "premium residential VPS as an asset" case.

## Key docs

- [README.md](README.md): Bilingual overview, one-line installer, architecture diagrams (single-node + dual-node).
- [docs/zh-CN/DEPLOYMENT.md](docs/zh-CN/DEPLOYMENT.md) · [docs/en/DEPLOYMENT.md](docs/en/DEPLOYMENT.md): Step-by-step deployment.
- [docs/zh-CN/DUAL-NODE.md](docs/zh-CN/DUAL-NODE.md) · [docs/en/DUAL-NODE.md](docs/en/DUAL-NODE.md): Dual-node + smart routing.
- [docs/zh-CN/SUBSCRIPTION.md](docs/zh-CN/SUBSCRIPTION.md) · [docs/en/SUBSCRIPTION.md](docs/en/SUBSCRIPTION.md): Subscription server design (Subscription-Userinfo header, traffic card, /healthz).
- [docs/zh-CN/TROUBLESHOOTING.md](docs/zh-CN/TROUBLESHOOTING.md) · [docs/en/TROUBLESHOOTING.md](docs/en/TROUBLESHOOTING.md): Why your Telegram is stalled, why OpenAI says "unsupported region", etc.
- [docs/zh-CN/CLIENTS.md](docs/zh-CN/CLIENTS.md) · [docs/en/CLIENTS.md](docs/en/CLIENTS.md): Client import (v2rayN / Clash Verge / sing-box / Shadowrocket).
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): Version history.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Threat model, secret denylist policy.

## What this toolkit deploys

- **sing-box** with **VLESS + Reality + xtls-rprx-vision** inbound on port 443 (no domain, no TLS cert needed)
- **Zero-dependency Python subscription server** returning a Clash-format profile with `Subscription-Userinfo` traffic header
- **Optional dual-node smart routing**: data-center "leaf" node polls the residential "aggregator" for status, returns a merged subscription with Clash rules sending OpenAI/Anthropic/Netflix/banking domains through residential, Telegram/Discord through the DC node
- **System tuning**: BBR, swap, journald log limits
- **Hardening**: UFW (only 443 + subscription port open), fail2ban
- **Operability**: systemd-timer daily encrypted config backup, `/healthz` endpoint, end-to-end self-check on every install
- **Idempotent installer**: re-running does not duplicate config or rotate secrets

## What this toolkit does NOT do

- ❌ Provide servers or residential IPs — you bring the VPS
- ❌ Run on CentOS 7, Alpine, OpenWRT (Ubuntu 22.04+ / Debian 12+ only by design)
- ❌ Set up domain-based TLS (Reality has no domain requirement; that's the whole point)
- ❌ Hide your egress IP (residential IP is the asset — it's *meant* to be visible)
- ❌ Bypass IP-based bans on services that detect VLESS itself (use a different tool)
- ❌ Manage multi-user accounting beyond the single-UUID model (`xray-core` / 3x-ui are better for that)

## Common questions

- **Why are Telegram file uploads stalling on my residential VPS?** Telegram soft-throttles residential subnets that have hosted bots in the past. Use the dual-node mode and route `geosite:telegram` through the data-center fallback.
- **Why does OpenAI think I'm in an unsupported region from my data-center VPS?** Because data-center IPs are flagged as commercial / VPN-likely. That's exactly why you want residential egress for OpenAI — the inverse of the Telegram problem.
- **Does Reality need a domain?** No. That's its main advantage over Trojan / V2Ray-TLS. You pick a high-reputation SNI to spoof (default: `addons.mozilla.org`).
- **Why GPL-3.0?** Inherited from sing-box's ecosystem norms. Use in proprietary products requires open-sourcing under GPL-3.0 or a commercial license arrangement.
- **Can I re-run the installer safely?** Yes — installer is idempotent and will not rotate UUIDs or Reality keys on re-run.

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/tytsxai/reality-resi-stack
- License: GPL-3.0
- Core: sing-box
- Platforms: Ubuntu 22.04+, Debian 12+
- Author: tytsxai
